items.title: Paddington 2
items.description: A warm, low-threat comfort movie with kindness, humor and a steady emotional landing.

items.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty
items.description: A visually spacious story about courage, identity and small steps toward a wider life.

items.title: Inside Out
items.description: A direct but playful way to reflect on mixed feelings and the purpose of sadness.

items.title: Chef
items.description: A food-and-family reset story that releases pressure through craft, music and connection.

items.title: The Grand Budapest Hotel
items.description: Fast, precise comedy with visual delight for a mind that wants novelty without heaviness.

items.title: The Comfort Book by Matt Haig
items.description: Short, compassionate passages that are easy to read when attention or energy is low.

items.title: Atomic Habits by James Clear
items.description: Simple behaviour systems for turning vague pressure into small, repeatable actions.

items.title: Big Magic by Elizabeth Gilbert
items.description: A light, energizing invitation to move curiosity into creative action.

items.title: Meditations by Marcus Aurelius
items.description: Short reflections that help separate what happened from what you choose next.

items.title: The Little Prince by Antoine de Saint-Exupery
items.description: A gentle, symbolic story for softer reflection and emotional simplicity.

items.title: Tiny Debug Joke
items.description: Why did the function feel calm? It finally returned.

items.title: Coffee Mood
items.description: My coffee tried meditation. Now it is less espresso, more depresso-no-more.

items.title: Anger Cooling
items.description: I told my temper to take five. It negotiated for three and a snack.

items.title: Surprise Note
items.description: I joined a surprise party planning group. Nobody expected me, so technically I passed.

items.title: Fear Grounding
items.description: My anxiety bought a planner. It scheduled worrying for later, then forgot the meeting.
